items.title: 11 Stocks That Have Cratered and Look Like Buys
items.description: Evercore strategist Julian Emanuel listed a bunch of examples.  A cohort of other healthcare stocks have been crushed as well, including providers of data-centric tools that pharmaceutical companies use to create new drugs.

items.title: UnitedHealth Stock: Is the Market Overreacting to Lower-Than-Expected Medicare Rates?
items.description: The stock has already declined 16% this year.

items.title: Ambulatory Surgical Centers Market to Reach Over US$ 142.6 Billion by 2033 Driven by Rising Outpatient Surgeries, Favorable Reimbursement, and Cost-Efficient Care Models | Astute Analytica
items.author: Astute Analytica
items.description: The global ambulatory surgical center (ASC) market is projected to grow from US$ 85.9 billion in 2024 to US$ 142.6 billion by 2033, driven by cost-effectiveness, favorable reimbursement policies, expanding CMS-approved procedures, and patient preference for outpatient surgeries. Physician-owned ASCs dominate with 59.56% market share, while North America leads globally with 26.46% share. Key growth factors include significant cost savings compared to hospital outpatient departments and technological advancements enabling more complex procedures.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Key market participant in the expanding ASC sector, benefiting from favorable regulatory environment and growing patient preference for outpatient procedures.
As a major healthcare payer and provider, positioned to benefit from ASC growth as these facilities reduce overall healthcare costs and improve operational efficiency in the healthcare system.

items.title: 2 Warren Buffett Stocks to Buy Hand Over Fist in 2026 and 1 to Avoid
items.author: Thomas Niel
items.description: The article analyzes three Berkshire Hathaway holdings: DaVita and Kraft Heinz are recommended as strong buys due to positive earnings surprises and undervaluation, while UnitedHealth Group should be avoided despite recent dips, as it trades at a premium valuation with a deteriorating growth story amid lower Medicare Advantage payment increases.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company beat quarterly earnings expectations and exceeded 2026 guidance with EPS between $13.60-$15. Trading at 9x forward earnings with potential for multiple expansion to historical 13-14x levels. Stock surged 30% post-earnings.

Trading at attractive 9x forward earnings discount to peers. Planned separation into high-growth and low-growth entities could unlock significant shareholder value, supported by precedent from Kellogg's successful 2023 split.

Stock declined from $350 to $280 after company walked back 2026 guidance due to lower-than-expected Medicare Advantage payment increases. Currently trades at 16x forward earnings premium to peers with continued multiple compression risk as growth story deteriorates.
Mentioned as the holding company with a 60-year track record of strong returns. Article focuses on analysis of its portfolio holdings rather than the company itself.
